summary: Drivers must run SDAM flow even when server description is equal to the last one
Add coefficient encoding detail to Decimal128 specification
description:
Opening this as a tracking ticket for [mongodb/specifications#795|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/pull/795]. This needs to be assigned a reviewer to determine if the contribution can be merged.

Previously reported as DOCS-13645, which was since closed.

summary: allowDiskUse option for find should be documented as only being supported in 4.4+
description:
Right now, the crud spec's documentation for the {{allowDiskUse}} field on {{FindOptions}} does not mention that the option is only supported in MongoDB 4.4+. It mentions that the option is ignored in servers < 3.2, but does not provide guidance on error handling as other options do.

Here is the relevant section:
{code}
* This option is sent only if the caller explicitly provides a value. The default
* is to not send a value. For servers < 3.2, this option is ignored and not sent
* as allowDiskUse does not exist in the OP_QUERY wire protocol. {code}
For comparison, the {{hint}} field in {{UpdateOptions}} which was introduced in 4.2 has the following guidance:
{code}
* This option is only supported by servers >= 4.2. Older servers >= 3.4 will report an error for using this option.
* For servers < 3.4, the driver MUST raise an error if the caller explicitly provides a value.
* For unacknowledged writes using OP_UPDATE or OP_MSG for servers < 4.2, the driver MUST raise an error if the caller explicitly provides a value.
{code}
{{allowDiskUse}} should probably be updated to match the guidance of {{hint}} to allow for consistent error messaging across options that were introduced in recent server versions.

Spec change: https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/8b1d6e9ff4a7dfd2a47d3fcf6e0ee6b2d98291a7

summary: Use runOn syntax to specify CRUD test requirements
Spec tests should use the same format for bulk writes
description:
There are three changes, the first two are breaking. 

The two breaking changes are in three command-monitoring tests: "bulkWrite.yml", "unacknowledgedBulkWrite.yml" and "insertMany.yml":

https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/bebf9b688f64afdec46a860f102bad0841b04c02

The format of the "requests" array and "ordered" option changes from this:
{noformat}
    operation:
      name: "bulkWrite"
      arguments:
        requests:
          - insertOne: 
              document: { _id: 4, x: 44 }
        ordered: false
{noformat}
To this:
{noformat}
    operation:
      name: "bulkWrite"
      arguments:
        requests:
          - name: "insertOne"
            arguments:
              document: { _id: 4, x: 44 }
        options:
          ordered: false
{noformat}
This brings Command Monitoring tests in line with the Retryable Writes and forthcoming Transactions tests.

The non-breaking change is in the Retryable Writes tests:

https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/c0ce96daac5661f2603bfa52c9b3f683c176a292

Before, the tests said in prose that all MongoClients should be created with retryWrites=true. Now, the tests include the following YAML:
{noformat}
        clientOptions:
            retryWrites: true
{noformat}
The clientOptions are the same for all Retryable Writes tests. Interpreting this field now will prepare you for the Transactions tests, which make real use of the field.

summary: Deprecate geoHaystack and geoSearch
Default OCSP to "off" for drivers that hard-fail when an OCSP responder is unavailable
description:
In order to prevent applications from experiencing connectivity issues when an OCSP responder becomes temporarily unavailable, we should update the spec to note that drivers whose TLS libraries hard-fail when an OCSP responder is unavailable should default to disabling OCSP.

Spec commit: [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/26b4ca576fef19de5727458d83baa60b08e6982e]

summary: Clarify that change stream command event expectations cover subset of all events
Reduce default keepalive time to align with Azure defaults
description:
From [~james.kovacs]:

According to our production notes (https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/administration/production-notes/#azure)...
The TCP idle timeout on the Azure load balancer is 240 seconds by default, which can cause it to silently drop connections if the TCP keepalive on your Azure systems is greater than this value. You should set tcp_keepalive_time to 120 to ameliorate this problem.

summary: Clarify that dollar-prefixed fields not matching known types are left as-is during parsing
description:
During a scope review for the "Mitigate pain of using field names with dots and dollars" server project, questions were raised about how the Extended JSON spec handles the case of parsing dollar-prefixed fields.

The [Parsers|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/extended-json.rst#parsers] section of the spec states:

bq. When parsing a JSON object other than the top-level object, the presence of a $-prefixed key indicates the object could be a type wrapper object as described in the Extended JSON Conversion table. In such a case, the parser MUST follow these rules, unless configured to allow Legacy Extended JSON, in which case it SHOULD follow these rules

It goes on to say that dollar-prefixed fields matching a known type shall be strictly validated against the structure defined in the spec; however, the spec does not address how dollar-prefixed fields not matching a known type code should be handled.

In the interest of the server project, such fields should be left as-is. This is a first step to ensuring compatibility with the project, and can be addressed before a future ticket to introduce some syntax for escaping dots/dollars in extended JSON (which will ultimately allow conflicts with types like $numberLong to be avoided entirely).

summary: aws_e2e_ec2.js should wait longer for EC2
Update sdam monitoring tests with directConnection uri option
description:
The tests in https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/tree/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/tests/monitoring should be reviewed for those that specify a single seed. These tests should have directConnection uri option explicitly added to the uri, to disambiguate the expected behavior with respect to forcing single topology or discovering topology.

-----

Spec change: https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/e0f32a668c514ef5268c92e334ec48ba7b962790

Existing tests that expect a direct connection are updated to have the directConnection=true URI option.

One test was added for monitoring a discovered standalone connection.

summary: waitQueueTimeoutMS description in URI options spec is incorrect
description:
The table in the URI options spec describes waitQueueTimeoutMS a
{quote}The maximum number of clients or connections able to be created by a pool at a given time
{quote}
This is actually the description of the maxPoolSize option.

summary: Pause connection pool when server becomes unknown
description:
Notes for implementers:
* A part of DRIVERS-1476 is actually an inherent part of the current improvement (DRIVERS-1103), some changes (especially tests) from DRIVERS-1476 may be implemented after implementing the current improvement.
* The following test and specification changes were filed as a result of issues arising when implementing the current improvement in some drivers, so it is recommended to grab / implement them as part of the current improvement:
** DRIVERS-1631
** DRIVERS-1647
** DRIVERS-1703
** DRIVERS-1722
** DRIVERS-1724
** DRIVERS-1726
** DRIVERS-1727
** DRIVERS-1804

summary: Start standalone with requireAPIVersion through mongo orchestration
description: Before sending language teams off to implement their instantiation of Versioned API, we would like to have an evergreen config that we can compare against.

summary: Test that Host header is present in OCSP HTTP requests
Improve ecs role imdsv2 checks
description: See DRIVERS-1284 for details.

summary: Test that Host header is present in OCSP HTTP requests
description:
HTTP requests made to OCSP responders must include the Host header.

Assertions were added to the mock OCSP responder to ensure a Host header is present in SERVER-49383. drivers-evergreen-tools will resync the responder so drivers can verify they are sending the Host header.

If drivers are not sending the Host header, they will fail OCSP tests that expect a revoked response (i.e. not a soft-fail). In particular, [Test 4|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/tree/db4c07bbe1dda78a020e567f71b1d2cabf221f74/source/ocsp-support/tests#integration-tests-permutations-to-be-tested] would fail.

*Drivers that are cloning drivers-evergreen-tools will not need to make any change unless they observe test failures.*

summary: Deprecate eval helpers
description: In MongoDB 3.0 the eval command was deprecated and it is removed in 4.0. Drivers must deprecate any helpers for the eval command and stop testing those helpers with MongoDB 4.0+. Deprecated helpers can be removed with the next semver major version bump of a driver.

summary: Benchmark data set deep_bson file is garbage data
description:
It appears that somehow the deep_bson.json file stored in the [extended BSON .tgz in the benchmarking spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/benchmarking/data/extended_bson.tgz] has been corrupted - the first few lines of the file for me show as

ã 'm[Ì{◊Æ‰HíÂ<ÔW4Ûÿ= -ÿj≠5ãµ÷öç˛˜uFdfeU∞jÍ•w∞@:êqoíAßππ˘±sÃ˝Üπ”ˇó€UÂ§‡„ﬂ˛%Ìp8‹.óøl?Ô∑€ÎÁ·Ù˛?h«˚vÛx>]◊≠›ˇr8^œ∑„ø˝ÂØ1Á◊mËzß¶¯N›:±S$Œæ¿◊¬˙yÊ/

And the rest is similar non-JSON garbage.

This appears to have been introduced via [#291|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/pull/291] when the data set was updated to use extJSON v2.

Looking at the data from the legacy archive, the old version of deep_bson doesn't seem to use any specific syntax that changed between extJSON v1 and v2 so I think we could just copy over the old version of the file.

That said, I think the introduction of this issue further justifies [~matt.broadstone]'s suggestion in the PR that we stop storing these files as tarballs and instead use .json files directly, as we then would've noticed this issue on the PR.

summary: Retry test failover operations until they succeed
Use shallow clone for driver repositories and submodules
Introduce spec test syntax for meta assertions (e.g. any value, not exists)
description:
Originally, the PR for SPEC-1210 included the following proposal for new syntax intended to replace use of {{42}} and {{null}} to indicate "any value" or "must not exist" during spec test runner document match assertions:

{noformat}
Asserting Nonexistent Fields
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Some expected documents may include ``{ $exists: false }`` as a field value.
When evaluating this, the test runner MUST assert that ``actual`` does not
contain the corresponding field. For instance, this may be used to assert that a
CommandStartedEvent's command document does not contain a ``writeConcern``
field.

Asserting Fields with Any Value
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Some expected documents may include ``{ $exists: true }`` as a field value. When
evaluating this, the test runner MUST assert that ``actual`` contains the
corresponding field (any BSON value is acceptable). For instance, this may be
used to assert that a document contains *some* value in its ``_id`` field.
{noformat}

Some conversation about this syntax occurred in [this PR thread|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/pull/453#discussion_r251638306].

[~daniel.aprahamian] pointed out that using MQL's {{$exists}} operator in this fashion, while fine for top-level fields, could lead to ambiguity in a nested context. For instance, the APM and Transaction spec tests use "any value" assertions on nested {{writeErrors[i].code}} and {{readConcern.afterClusterTime}} fields, respectively.

[~shane.harvey] suggested using a custom operator name, such as "$assertExists". This lead me to consider that a flexible solution may be to come up with one custom name (e.g. "$assert") and then allow a subset of relevant query operators such as {{$exists}} and {{$type}} (allowing the benefit of {{$exists: true}} with additional strictness to expect a particular BSON type).

In any event, this syntax was not required for any existing CRUD tests. Rather, it aligns with the drivers team's long-term goal of consolidating all spec test formats. As such, I've removed the snippet above from the SPEC-1210 PR and created this ticket to address this down the line.

summary: Spec tests should use a consistent format for CRUD options
description:
While implementing SPEC-1142, I came across an inconsistency with how the spec tests express options for CRUD tests (and possible others, such as APM and retryable writes).

This is noticed when comparing the following two tests:

 * [crud/tests/write/bulkWrite-arrayFilters.yml|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/b020a754aea42693709959a834b644b796d26f91/source/crud/tests/write/bulkWrite-arrayFilters.yml#L32]
 * [crud/tests/write/updateOne-arrayFilters.yml|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/b020a754aea42693709959a834b644b796d26f91/source/crud/tests/write/updateOne-arrayFilters.yml#L16]

The options for single-statement methods are expressed alongside their positional arguments. In the {{updateOne()}} example, {{filter}}, {{update}}, and {{arrayFilters}} are all keys within the same document; however, in {{bulkWrite}}, the {{ordered}} option is nested within an {{options}} key. The {{options}} and {{requests}} keys are within the same document.

Note: this is not (yet) a problem for [CRUD's insertMany.yml|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/b020a754aea42693709959a834b644b796d26f91/source/crud/tests/write/insertMany.yml] because it only has a single test that does not express any options. The [APM's inserMany.yml|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/b020a754aea42693709959a834b644b796d26f91/source/command-monitoring/tests/insertMany.yml#L61] does include a test with "options: \{ ordered: false \}"; however, I'd only like to focus on standardizing the CRUD spec tests in this ticket. APM tests may be updated at a later time when we standardize on a single format for integration tests (following SPEC-1144).

In order to make the CRUD tests follow a consistent format, I propose that we standardize on one of the following:

 * Nest all options under an "options" key (as is currently done for {{bulkWrite()}}) and strictly adhere to the method prototypes in the CRUD spec (i.e. required parameters are positional, and all options are contained within a final, optional "options" parameter). This comes at the expense of changing most all of the single-statement CRUD spec tests. This change would not apply to {{requests}} within the {{bulkWrite()}} tests due to how the [bulk write models|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/crud/crud.rst#bulk-write-models] are defined (i.e. no "options" nesting), which might be confusing.
 * Do not nest any options under an "options" key. This would make {{bulkWrite()}} spec tests consistent with the other single-statement tests and require minimal changes. We could include a note in the test README to explain that the tests omit "options" nesting in the interest of conciseness.

The pedant in me prefers the first option but I anticipate folks may prefer the second since it would require minimal changes.

summary: mongo-orchestration incompatible with Python 3.8
description:
I have Python 3.8.5 installed locally and installed mongo-orchestration with:

{noformat}
pip install mongo-orchestration
{noformat}

Attempting to start MO resulted in the following error:

{noformat}
$ mongo-orchestration --no-fork start
Starting Mongo Orchestration on port 8889...
Bottle v0.12.19 server starting up (using CherryPyServer())...
Listening on http://localhost:8889/
Hit Ctrl-C to quit.

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/jmikola/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/mongo_orchestration/server.py", line 132, in run
    run(get_app(), host=self.args.bind, port=self.args.port,
  File "/home/jmikola/.local/bin/bottle.py", line 3137, in run
    server.run(app)
  File "/home/jmikola/.local/bin/bottle.py", line 2795, in run
    from cherrypy import wsgiserver
  File "/home/jmikola/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/cherrypy/__init__.py", line 64, in <module>
    from cherrypy._cperror import HTTPError, HTTPRedirect, InternalRedirect
  File "/home/jmikola/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/cherrypy/_cperror.py", line 118, in <module>
    from cgi import escape as _escape
ImportError: cannot import name 'escape' from 'cgi' (/usr/lib/python3.8/cgi.py)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/jmikola/.local/bin/mongo-orchestration", line 8, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "/home/jmikola/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/mongo_orchestration/server.py", line 185, in main
    daemon.run()
  File "/home/jmikola/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/mongo_orchestration/server.py", line 132, in run
    run(get_app(), host=self.args.bind, port=self.args.port,
  File "/home/jmikola/.local/bin/bottle.py", line 3137, in run
    server.run(app)
  File "/home/jmikola/.local/bin/bottle.py", line 2795, in run
    from cherrypy import wsgiserver
  File "/home/jmikola/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/cherrypy/__init__.py", line 64, in <module>
    from cherrypy._cperror import HTTPError, HTTPRedirect, InternalRedirect
  File "/home/jmikola/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/cherrypy/_cperror.py", line 118, in <module>
    from cgi import escape as _escape
ImportError: cannot import name 'escape' from 'cgi' (/usr/lib/python3.8/cgi.py)
{noformat}

Per 3.8's [API and Feature Removals|https://docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.8.html#api-and-feature-removals]:

{quote}
parse_qs, parse_qsl, and escape are removed from the cgi module. They are deprecated in Python 3.2 or older. They should be imported from the urllib.parse and html modules instead.
{quote}

summary: Add 4.4 wire version to the list
description: https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/wireversion-featurelist.rst is missing the wire version for 4.4 (9).

summary: Revisions to "Issues and Help" pages of drivers documentation
description:
Example: https://mongodb.github.io/mongo-csharp-driver/2.2/issues_help/
1) doesn't include links to support team (support.mongodb.com)
2) link to community forum is no longer accurate (it's now community.mongodb.com)
3) we could include a link to file a feature request (feedback.mongodb.com)

We should go through all drivers and make sure any help/assistance pages have the correct links/directions.

summary: Do not repeatedly resume if getMore receives the same error
description:
The [change streams spec resumable criteria is broad|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/197f446c82b271c4f42de3e86af8f2246a5d6b2f/source/change-streams/change-streams.rst#resumable-error], considering all server errors resumable except a few exclusions, or errors with a {{NonResumableChangeStreamError}} label.

Drivers resume once, but we incorrectly assume that if we resume due to an error received on a getMore command, and succeed in sending the aggregate during resume, it is worthwhile to attempt the next getMore command. It is possible for getMores to consistently fail, even though aggregate succeeds.

I believe this warrants modifying either the criteria for resume logic (perhaps making it a whitelist instead of a blacklist), or modifying how we repeat resuming. E.g. perhaps we consider an error of the first getMore to be a failure to resume.

Spec commit: [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/f93d78191f3db2898a59013a7ed5650352ef6da8]

summary: Work around failover test failing due to cluster state
description:
The test failover operation can fail due to hidden atlas state differing from published state, as described in https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/PRODTRIAGE-1232. https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/DRIVERS-1585 appears to have installed a workaround for this but there are still failures, perhaps the waits need to be made longer.



{code:java}

summary: Update workload executor steps for generating result.json
description: Currently, step 10 in [the workload executor spec|https://mongodb-labs.github.io/drivers-atlas-testing/spec-workload-executor.html] says that numFailures and numErrors should be -1 when they're not specified in options, when they should just be 0.

summary: mongodb-logs.tar.gz does not contain mongod logs
description: I noticed the downloads for a completed task only include logs for the three mongos hosts and not the actual mongods. Since we have tests that are specifically interacting with replica sets (e.g. failovers), I assume the mongod logs are also of interest.

summary: "aggregate with multiple batches works" unified CRUD test does not pass on all server releases
description:
[This test|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/crud/tests/unified/aggregate.yml#L28] does not pass on all server release/topology types. See [this patch|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/version/6095d6a22a60ed1e2e4646ea] for details.

The test asserts that an aggregate command with batch size one that expects three documents in the result will execute an aggregate command followed by two getMore commands (one command for each expected document in the result). But most server releases actually require an extra getMore command before returning a reply with a zero cursorId.

The specification says:
{quote}If test.expectEvents is specified, for each object therein the test runner MUST assert that the number and sequence of expected events match the number and sequence of actual events observed on the specified client.
{quote}
The Java driver fails the test against some server releases when comparing number of expected (3) vs actual (4) command events.

I'm not sure how other drivers are passing this test consistently on all supported server releases and topology types. There must be some difference in test runner implementations/spec interpretation.

summary: Fix license file at https://github.com/mongodb/specifications
description:
h2. Description
Apologies if I've designated the wrong categories here.  

The repo at [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications] correctly lists CC A-NC-SA as the license in the README file: "All the specs in this repository are available under the [Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 United States License|https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/3.0/us/]."

However, the actual license file thats included is instead CC Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0 Unported.  

Could you please fix the license file so it matches the README?

summary: Drivers should use majority write concern when retrying commitTransaction
writeError missing index in test file
description:
In the "writeErrors field is ignored" test in the SDAM errors spec tests, [a write error is specified that omits the {{index}} field|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/tests/errors/write_errors_ignored.yml#L40], which makes it harder to deserialize the test in statically typed languages. This should be updated to include an index.

Also, the write error currently uses the "command" error type, though it isn't really a command error since it has {{ok: 1}}. We should also consider updating the format to support a new {{bulk}} or {{write}} error type for these cases.

summary: Ensure that getMore right after a resume is retried in changestreams
description:
Spec change is [here|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/9ec7093cd5b4f9bfef8d4353d07cd51cf322bd98]

A couple of notes on this:

# it includes a YAML test (Thanks Shane for the suggestion)
# The YAML test requires use of a failpoint (the first in the change streams test, but exactly the same structure as transactions spec tests and others)
# The YAML test requires use of a changeStreamOption: batchSize (also the first in the change streams tests.  all other tests have an empty options map)

summary: SDAM section order got messed up
description:
On https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/server-discovery-and-monitoring.rst the headings now are:

- TopologyType Single
- TopologyType LoadBalanced
- Verifying setName with TopologyType Single

The setName in single topology section is now attached to load balanced topology type instead of being under the single topology type.

Also, "Checking wire protocol compatibility" section doesn't state the requirements for the load balanced topologies despite being under the load balanced topology heading.

summary: listCollections output should allow user to recreate time-series collections
Replace usages of 'acceptAPIVersion2' with 'acceptApiVersion2'
description:
{panel:title=Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
The 'acceptAPIVersion2' server parameter has been renamed to 'acceptApiVersion2'.
{panel}
h2. Description of Linked Ticket

4.9.0-alpha5 server offers the following parameters:
{code} "acceptAPIVersion2"=>false,
 "requireApiVersion"=>false,
{code}
As a user of the server I would like the server to use consistent case for related parameters, i.e. either use API or Api for both (and all of the other API version-related parameters).
h2. Drivers Changes Needed

Tests should be synced to incorporate the changes from [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/e728dfbc77e5e3f00a9b8b4301ec59fe14d87eda|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/e728dfbc77e5e3f00a9b8b4301ec59fe14d87eda].

summary: Implement builder for $count accumulator
Bump maxWireVersion for MongoDB 5.0
description: The maxWireVersion for MongoDB 5.0 is 13.  Drivers should bump their maxWireVersion to 13 for purposes of the [version compatibility check|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/server-discovery-and-monitoring.rst#checking-wire-protocol-compatibility].

summary: Require passing Stable API options to getMore and transaction-continuing commands
Do not require MONGODB_URI in run-load-balancer.sh when stopping
description:
{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#21313c|titleBGColor=#21313c|titleColor=#fafbfc|bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*
When stopping the load balancer with {{run-load-balancer.sh stop}} the script currently requires MONGODB_URI to be defined.

h3. *Motivation*

This is easy to accidentally omit if {{run-load-balancer.sh stop}} is being run as part of a teardown script that does not fail the task on error. This could result in the haproxy process continuing to run. This was fixed this in the Go driver's evergreen here: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/pull/669#discussion_r633866550. I almost introduced this in the C driver's evergreen config: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/808/files#r662671620

h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
This is only test improvements, has an easy workaround, and is not urgent.

{panel}

summary: Allow flexible order when evaluating runOnRequirements
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*
As Andreas noted in [mongodb/mongo-php-library#854|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/pull/854#discussion_r687446388], the unified test format specifies a particular order for evaluating runOnRequirements. It may not be necessary to be strict about the order of evaluation, and at the very least we can re-order certain checks to be more efficient. For example, checking an environment variable (e.g. serverless) is quite cheap and could be done before communicating with any server to check something like a topology or version.

h3. *Motivation*

h4. Who is the affected end user?
Drivers implementations of unified test runner.

h4. How does this affect the end user?
Inefficient test execution.

h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?
Every check of runOnRequirements.

h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?
Inefficiency.

h4. Is this issue urgent?
No.

h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No.

h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
Yes.
{panel}

summary: Allow hinting the delete command
Versioned API database aggregation test only works on 4.9+ servers
description:
In https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/DRIVERS-1505 the database aggregation test was changed from expecting success to expecting failure, following the server change in https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/SERVER-51618.

However the server change was made in 4.9.0, but the test continues to specify 4.7.0 as the minimum version. In 4.7.0 the operation succeeds and the test therefore fails.

Drivers will need to sync the {{source/versioned-api/tests/*}} test files to stop testing on 4.7 servers.

summary: Calling cursor.next() and cursor.close() concurrently in Load Balanced mode uses the same connection simultaneously
description:
TLDR: Calling cursor.next() and cursor.close() concurrently in LB mode leads to using the pinned connection simultaneously for two operations.

When a cursor is pinned in Load Balanced mode and the app calls cursor.close() the driver is required to send killCursors on the same connection. But what happens if the app calls close() concurrently while iterating the cursor? Without any safe guards this leads to unexpected simultaneous use of the underlying connection. We can’t read/write to the same connection in two threads.

In particular I’m wondering about change stream cursors. In python we’ve advocated for calling close() concurrently in order to cancel the ChangeStream.next() call which can potentially block forever. Currently, the concurrent close() call will check out a new connection for the killCursors request, kill the cursor, and short circuit the loop in next().

Potential solutions:
- Add documentation that applications cannot use a cursor concurrently. The Go driver documents this on their Cursor api already.
- Add a mutex to every pinned Cursor to synchronize connection access.

summary: CMAP - change the requirement for total connections if minPoolSize is set and a pool is in the "ready" state
description:
[CMAP 1.4.0|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/28c6c7bf0deb10cf3b8a47c0c53d1720be78fc11/source/connection-monitoring-and-pooling/connection-monitoring-and-pooling.rst] requires
{quote}If minPoolSize is set, the Connection Pool MUST have at least minPoolSize total Connections while it is "ready". {quote}

With regard to the "paused"/"ready" states, this requirement would not only require the method {{ready()}} to block until the pool is populated, but would also need another internal state that allows populating a pool (just like it is allowed in the "ready" state), but does not allow checking out connections (if this were allowed, the {{checkOut()}} method could have observed that there are less than {{minPoolSize}} total connections by being blocked when it is not supposed to).

Another consideration is that a connection may be closed by the {{checkIn(connection: Connection)}} method because it's perishable, and this fact makes implementing the requirement impossible.

It appears to me that all we can require is that a pool SHOULD (or MUST strive to?) have at least minPoolSize total connections.

[~patrick.freed] confirmed, that this requirement does not express the intent.

summary: When Astrolabe run fails due to issue with Atlas, color it lavender to indicate a setup failure
description: See https://github.com/evergreen-ci/evergreen/wiki/Project-Configuration-Files#command-failure-colors
